content.title: The Exploration of Mars
content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲述了意大利天文学家乔瓦尼·斯卡帕雷利和美国天文学家珀西瓦尔·洛威尔对火星表面的观测和研究，洛威尔认为火星上存在一座巨大的灌溉渠网，用于为火星人种植庄稼。', 'B': '19世纪末，人们开始相信火星上有智慧生命，这种观念在H.G.威尔斯的《世界大战》和埃德加·赖斯·伯勒斯的火星系列小说中得到了进一步加强，而奥森·威尔斯在1938年的广播节目《世界大战》更是引发了美国的大规模恐慌，人们纷纷逃离家中。', 'C': '探测器对于我们了解其他行星非常重要，最近的许多知识都来自这些进入太空的机器人任务。', 'D': '这段话主要讲了地球上发现的火星陨石引发了对以上分析的怀疑，火星陨石中发现的磁铁矿只在地球上与某些微生物有关联，而火星陨石中也存在类似地球细菌的矿化结构，但纳米细菌的存在本身仍然存在争议。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了火星缺乏全球磁场导致宇宙辐射和太阳辐射对行星造成潜在威胁，进而导致火星大气层逐渐消失，从而限制了火星上可能存在的生命的深度范围，并且火星的磁场消失可能在火星气候变化过程中起到了重要作用。', 'F': 'NASA的最近任务主要关注另一个问题：火星在古代是否有液态水的湖泊或海洋。科学家们发现了赤铁矿，这是一种在水存在的情况下形成的矿物。因此，2004年火星探测车的任务不是寻找现在或过去的生命，而是寻找火星表面古代时期的液态水证据。液态水对地球生命和地球物种通常进行的新陈代谢是必需的，但在火星表面的现有低大气压和温度下，除了在最低阴影高度的短时间内，液态水无法存在，且液态水不会出现在表面本身。2004年3月，NASA宣布其火星探测车机会号发现了火星在古代时期是一个湿润的星球的证据。这引起了人们对今天在火星上可能发现过去生命的希望。欧洲航天局在2004年1月证实，火星快车号轨道飞行器直接探测到了火星南极的巨大水冰储量。', 'G': '研究人员发现了阿塔卡马沙漠地下两米处的微生物“绿洲”，这对于在火星等类似地下环境中寻找生命迹象的探测器SOLID具有重要意义。', 'H': '这段话主要讲述了西班牙科学家提出的一个有趣的假设：如果火星上的样本能够像地球生命一样使用DNA作为遗传密码，那么DNA这种高度专门化和复杂的分子极不可能在两个行星上分别独立进化，这表明火星和地球生命必然有共同的起源。如果这种情况被证实，我们将不得不面对一个合乎逻辑的结论：我们都是火星人。如果不是这样，我们将继续寻找生命的迹象。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: In 1877, Giovanni Schiaparelli, an Italian astronomer, made drawings and maps of the Martian surface that suggested strange features. The images from telescopes at this time were not as sharp as today's. Schiaparelli said he could see a network of lines, or canali. In 1894, an American astronomer, Percival Lowell, made a series of observations of Mars from his own observations of Mars from his own observatory at Flagstaff, Arizona, USA. Lowell was convinced a great network of canals had been dug to irrigate crops for the Martian race! He suggested that each canal had fertile vegetation on either side, making them noticeable from Earth. Drawings and globes he made show a network of canals and oases all over the planet.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The idea that there was intelligent life on Mars gained strength in the late 19th century. In 1898, H.G. Wells wrote a science fiction classic, The War of the Worlds about an invading force of Martians who try to conquer Earth. They use highly advanced technology (advanced for 1898) to crush human resistance in their path. In 1917, Edgar Rice Burroughs wrote the first in a series of 11 novels about Mars. Strange beings and rampaging Martian monsters gripped the public's imagination. A radio broadcast by Orson Welles on Halloween night in 1938 of The War of the Worlds caused widespread panic across America. People ran into the streets in their pyjamas-millions believed the dramatic reports of a Martian invasion.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Probes are very important to our understanding of other planets. Much of our recent knowledge comes from these robotic missions into space. The first images sent back from Mars came from Mariner 4 in July 1965. They showed a cratered and barren landscape, more like the surface of our moon than Earth. In 1969, Mariners 6 and 7 were launched and took 200 photographs of Mars's southern hemisphere and pole on fly-by missions. But these showed little more information. In 1971, Mariner 9's mission was to orbit the planet every 12 hours. In 1975, The USA sent two Viking probes to the planet, each with a lander and an orbiter. The Landers had sampler arms to scoop up Maritain rocks and did experiments to try and find signs of life. Although no life was found, they sent back the first colour pictures of the planet's surface and atmosphere from pivoting cameras.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The Martian meteorite found in Earth aroused doubts to the above analysis. ALH84001 meteorite was discovered in December 1984 in Antarctica, by members of the ANSMET project; The sample was ejected from Mars about 17 million years ago and spent 11,000 years in or on the Antarctic ice sheets. Composition analysis by NASA revealed a kind of magnetite that on Earth, is only found in association with certain microorganisms. Some structures resembling the mineralized casts of terrestrial bacteria and their appendages fibrils or by-products occur in the rims of carbonate globules and pre-terrestrial aqueous alteration regions. The size and shape of the objects is consistent with Earthly fossilized nanobacteria but the existence of nanobacteria itself is still controversial.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: In 1965, the Mariner 4 probe discovered that Mars had no global magnetic field that would protect the planet from potentially life-threatening cosmic radiation and solar radiation; observations made in the late 1990s by the Mars Global Surveyor confirmed this discovery. Scientists speculate that the lack of magnetic shielding helped the solar wind blow away much of Mars's atmosphere over the course of several billion years. After mapping cosmic radiation levels at various depths on Mars, researchers have concluded that any life within the first several meters of the planet's surface would be killed by lethal doses of cosmic radiation. In 2007, it was calculated that DNA and RNA damage by cosmic radiation would limit life on Mars to depths greater than 7.5 metres below the planet's surface. Therefore, the best potential locations for discovering life on Mars may be at subsurface environments that have not been studied yet. Disappearance of the magnetic field may played an significant role in the process of Martian climate change. According to the valuation of the scientists, the climate of Mars gradually transits from warm and wet to cold and dry after magnetic field vanished.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text:
NASA's recent missions have focused on another question: whether Mars held lakes or oceans of liquid water on its surface in the ancient past. Scientists have found hematite, a mineral that forms in the presence of water. Thus, the mission of the Mars Exploration Rovers of 2004 was not to look for present or past life, but for evidence of liquid water on the surface of Mars in the planet's ancient past. Liquid water, necessary for Earth life and for metabolism as generally conducted by species on Earth, cannot exist on the surface of Mars under its present low atmospheric pressure and temperature, except at the lowest shaded elevations for short periods and liquid water does not appear at the surface itself. In March 2004, NASA announced that its rover Opportunity had discovered evidence that Mars was, in the ancient past, a wet planet. This had raised hopes that evidence of past life might be found on the planet today. ESA confirmed that the Mars Express orbiter had directly detected huge reserves of water ice at
Mars' south pole in January 2004.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Researchers from the Center of Astrobiology (Spain) and the Catholic University of the North in Chile have found an 'oasis' of microorganisms two meters below the surface of the Atacama Desert, SOLID, a detector for signs of life which could be used in environments similar to subsoil on Mars. "We have named it a 'microbial oasis' because we found microorganisms developing in a habitat that was rich in rock salt and other highly hygroscopic compounds that absorb water" explained Victor Parro, researcher from the Center of Astrobiology in Spain. "If there are similar microbes on Mars or remains in similar conditions to the ones we have found in Atacama, we could detect them with instruments like SOLID" Parro highlighted.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Even more intriguing, however, is the alternative scenario by Spanish scientists: If those samples could be found to that use DNA, as Earthly life does, as their genetic code. It is extremely unlikely that such a highly specialised, complex molecule like DNA could have evolved separately on the two planets, indicating that there must be a common origin for Martian and Earthly life. Life based on DNA first appeared on Mars and then spread to Earth, where it then evolved into the myriad forms of plants and creatures that exist today. If this was found to be the case, we would have to face the logical conclusion: we are all Martian. If not, we would continue to search the life of signs.

同样地，我们可以找到与"Earth"相关的句子："Even more intriguing, however, is the alternative scenario by Spanish scientists: If those samples could be found to that use DNA, as Earthly life does, as their genetic code." 和 "It is extremely unlikely that such a highly specialised, complex molecule like DNA could have evolved separately on the two planets, indicating that there must be a common origin for Martian and Earthly life." 这些句子表明地球上的生命和火星上的生命有共同的起源。

因此，根据题干中的"Mars and Earth may share the same life origin"，我们可以得出结论，本题对应的段落是H段落。

questions.analysis:
题干要求我们描述了Percival Lowell在这篇文章中如何描述火星。为了找到答案，我们可以注意到以下几点：

首先，我们可以通过定位词"Percival Lowell"来找到相关信息。在原文中，提到了"an American astronomer, Percival Lowell"，这就是我们要找的人物。

content.title: Global warming: Prevent poles from melting
content.summary: {'A': '我们对化石燃料的依赖以及已经释放到大气中的二氧化碳的数量如此之大，以至于大多数气候科学家都认为全球变暖是不可避免的，我们能做的最好的就是将其保持在一个合理的水平，而即使这也是一个艰巨的任务。\n目前，唯一一个严肃考虑的解决方案是减少碳排放，但只有少数几个国家在这方面取得了重大进展，大多数国家在阻止增长速度上甚至都很困难，更不用说逆转了。因此，越来越多的科学家开始探索替代方案，他们以地球工程学为旗帜，通常被定义为有意识的大规模环境操控。', 'B': '地球工程学在小范围内已经被证明有效，例如在莫斯科的五一游行中，飞机释放干冰、碘化银和水泥粉末来驱散云层，而现在提出的许多方案则旨在减少阳光照射地球的量。其中一种方案是通过向北极上空的平流层释放气溶胶喷雾来创造二氧化硫云，从而实现地球的整体降温，这个想法是基于历史上的火山爆发，例如1991年菲律宾的皮纳图博火山爆发，导致全球气温短期降低了0.5°C，气溶胶可以通过炮兵、高空飞机或气球进行释放。', 'C': '该段落主要讲述了一些关于逆转极地融化的方案，包括喷水加强冰盖以及使用加固的电缆阻止冰流进入海洋等，其中芬兰科学家Veil Albert Kallio认为使用电缆阻止冰流是不切实际的，但可以使用类似悬索桥的电缆来改变冰的南向运动方向。', 'D': '俄罗斯正在研究一些地球工程的想法，包括种植白桦树以增加反射阳光的数量，利用破冰船在北极和南极的秋季故意破坏和散布沿海海冰，以及改变河流的流向以增加冷水流入冰冻区域，以减缓全球变暖。', 'E': '总结：这段话主要讨论了地球工程的实施可能性以及其可能带来的副作用和风险。', 'F': '主要讲述了地球工程学被主流科学界接受的主要原因是大多数研究人员对政治家们达成协议并实施必要的碳减排措施缺乏信心，即使是领先的保护组织也认为这个问题值得探索。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text:
Such is our dependence on fossil fuels, and such the volume of carbon dioxide we have already released into the atmosphere, that most climate scientists agree that significant global warming is now inevitable – the best we can hope to do is keep it at a reasonable level, and even that going to be an uphill task.
At present, the only serious option on the table for doing this is cutting back on our carbon emissions, but a few countries are making major strides in this regard, the majority are having great difficulty even stemming the rate of increase, let alone reversing Consequently, an increasing number of scientists are beginning to explore the alternatives. They under the banner of geoengineering generally defined as the intentional large-scale manipulation of the environment.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text:
Geoengineering has been shown to work, at least on a small, localised scale, for decades. May Day parades in Moscow have taken place under clear blue skies, aircraft having deposited dry ice, silver iodide (m $1) and cement powder to disperse clouds. Many of the schemes now suggested look to do the opposite, and reduce the amount of sunlight reaching the planet.
One scheme focuses on achieving a general cooling of the Earth and involves the concept of releasing aerosol sprays into the stratosphere above the Arctic to create clouds of sulphur dioxide, which would, in turn, lead to a global dimming. The idea is modelled on historical volcanic explosions, such as that of Mount Pinatubo in the Philippines in 1991; which led to a short-term cooling of global temperatures by 0.5°c. The aerosols could be delivered by artillery, high-flying aircraft or balloons.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text:
Instead of concentrating on global cooling, other schemes look specifically at reversing the melting at the poles. One idea is to bolster an ice cap by spraying it with water. Using pumps to carry water from below the sea ice the spray would come out as snow or ice particles, producing thicker sea ice with a higher albedo (the ratio of sunlight reflected from a surface) to reflect summer radiation. Scientists have also scrutinised whether it is possible to block ice flow in Greenland with cables which have been reinforced, preventing icebergs from moving into the sea.
Veil Albert Kallio, a Finnish scientist, says that such an idea is impractical, because the force of the ice would ultimately snap the cables and rapidly release a large quantity of frozen ice into the sea. However, Kallio believes that the sort of cables used in suspension bridges could potentially be used to divert, rather than halt, the southward movement of ice from
Spitsbergen. It would stop the ice moving south, and local currents would see them float northwards' he says.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text:
A number of geoengineering ideas are currently being examined in the Russian Arctic. These include planting millions of birch trees: the thinking, according to Kallio, is that their white bark would increase the amount of reflected sunlight. The loss of their leaves in winter would also enable the snow to reflect radiation. In contrast, the native evergreen pines tend to shade the snow and absorb radiation.
Using ice-breaking vessels to deliberately break up and scatter coastal sea ice in both Arctic and Antarctic waters in their respective autumns, and diverting Russian rivers to increase cold-water flow to ice-forming areas, could also be used to slow down warming, Kallio says. 1 You would need the wind to blow the right way, but in the right conditions, by letting ice float free and head north, you would enhance ice growth.'

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text:
But will such ideas ever be implemented? The major counter-arguments to geoengineering schemes are, first, that they are a 'cop-out' that allow US to continue living the way we do, rather than reducing carbon emissions; and, second, even if they do work, would the side-effects outweigh the advantages? Then there's the daunting prospect of upkeep and repair of any scheme as well as the consequences of a technical failure. 'I think all of US agree that if we were to end geoengineering on a given day, then the planet would return to its pre-engineered condition very rapidly, and probably within 10 to 20 years' says Dr Phil Rasch, chief scientist for climate change at the US-based Pacific Northwest National Laboratory.
That's certainly something to worry about. I would consider geoengineering as a strategy to employ only we manage the conversion to a non-fossil- fuel economy. 'The risk with geoengineering projects is that you can "overshoot",' says Dr Dan hunt, from the University of Bristol. 'You may bring global temperatures back to pre-industrial levels, but the risk is that the poles will still be warmer than they should be and the tropics be cooler than before industrialization.'

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text:
The main reason why geoengineering is countenanced by the mainstream scientific community is that most researchers have little faith in the of politicians to agree – and then bring in the necessary carbon cuts. Even leading conservation organisations believe the subject worth exploring. As Dr Martin Sommerkorn, a climate change advisor says.'
But human-induced climate change has brought humanity to a position where it important not to exclude thinking thoroughly about this topic and its possibilities despite the potential drawbacks. If, over the coming years, the science US about an ever-increased climate sensitivity of the planet and this isn't unrealistic – then we may be best served by not having to start our thinking from scratch.

questions.title: the existence of geoengineering projects distracting from the real task of changing the way we live
questions.analysis:
题干中提到了"the existence of geoengineering projects distracting from the real task of changing the way we live"，我们可以将其中的关键词进行同义替换，即"geoengineering projects"可以替换为"geoengineering schemes"，"distracting from"可以替换为"cop-out"，"the real task of changing the way we live"可以替换为"reducing carbon emissions"。

在原文中，我们可以找到与题干相关的句子："The major counter-arguments to geoengineering schemes are, first, that they are a ‘cop-out’ that allow US to continue living the way we do, rather than reducing carbon emissions." 这句话中提到了geoengineering schemes是一种"cop-out"，即一种逃避责任的行为，它允许我们继续过着我们现在的生活方式，而不是减少碳排放。这与题干中提到的"the existence of geoengineering projects distracting from the real task of changing the way we live"是相符的。

content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲述了斯里兰卡南部干旱地区蒙纳拉加拉地区的一个村庄Muthukandiya，由于干旱导致的水源短缺，导致当地的稻米和淡水鱼供应减少，许多当地产业关闭，村民纷纷前往城镇寻找工作。', 'B': '这段话主要讲述了穆图坎迪亚村民的生活情况，他们依靠附近的甘蔗种植园打工维持生计，由于缺乏灌溉系统，他们的农作物产量不足以支持大部分家庭，村庄经济严重依赖于雨水，而且由于缺水问题，妇女和儿童每天可能要花费数小时步行三英里（五公里）去取水。', 'C': '1998年，南亚实用行动组织与该地区的社区讨论了水问题，并采取了一项基于低成本的“雨水收集”技术的干旱缓解举措，该技术已在斯里兰卡和该地区其他地方使用，通过槽和管道收集和储存屋顶上的雨水。', 'D': '尽管有着源自公元前三世纪的本土雨水收集和灌溉系统的传统，但现代决策者经常忽视这些技术的价值，直到最近官员们才对家庭级结构产生了浓厚兴趣。然而，政府和其他项目在概念和应用上都是自上而下的，免费安装水箱却没有提供建造和维护所需技能的培训。南亚实用行动组织的项目有意采取了不同的方法，旨在在建筑师和水箱使用者中建立本地技能基础，并创建结构和系统，使社区能够管理自己的雨水收集计划。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了Muthukandiya社区通过雨水收集技术解决了水问题，并且通过培训和建立社区组织来确保水质和水量的提高。\n', 'F': '这段话主要讲述了一个村庄中的寡妇Nandawathie如何利用雨水收集的机会改善了她家庭的生活，包括种植蔬菜、开设小店、安装太阳能以及改善孩子的健康和教育。', 'G': '这段话主要讲述了在短期和小规模上，该项目取得了明显的成功，但挑战在于使这样的倡议可持续并扩大其覆盖范围。', 'H': '该段落主要讲述了非结构性工作的挑战，特别是财务和组织方面的挑战，以及如何通过解释来克服这些挑战。', 'I': '这段话主要讲述了Muthkandiya计划作为一个示范项目，旨在通过雨水收集展示基于社区的干旱缓解的可行性，并且其他组织也开始采用相同的方法进行自己的项目，同时正在调查引入更大水箱的可行性。', 'J': '实施可持续的基于社区的项目需要大量的努力和耐心，才能产生兴趣、发展技能和组织管理结构，而在没有外部支持的情况下，雨水收集技术在该地区的村庄中迅速自发地推广可能需要一段时间。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Muthukandiya is a village in Moneragala district, one of the drought-stricken areas in the "dry zone" of southern Sri Lanka, where half the country's population of 18 million lives. Rainfall in the area varies greatly from year to year, often bringing extreme dry spells in between monsoons. But this drought was much worse than usual. Despite some rain in November, only half of Moneragala's 1,400 tube wells were in working order by March. The drought devastated supplies of rice and freshwater fish, the staple diet of inland villages. Many local industries closed down and villagers headed for the towns in search of work.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The villagers of muthukandiya arrived in the 1970s as part of a government resettlement scheme. Each family was given six acres of land, with no irrigation system. Because crop production, which relies entirely on rainfall, is insufficient to support most families, the village economy relies on men and women working as day-laborers in nearby sugar-cane plantations. Three wells have been dug to provide domestic water, but these run dry for much of the year. Women and children may spend several hours each day walking up to three miles (five kilometers) to fetch water for drinking, washing, and cooking.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: In 1998, communities in the district discussed water problems with Practical Action South Asia. What followed was a drought mitigation initiative based on a low-cost "rainwater harvesting"technology already used in Sri Lanka and elsewhere in the region. It uses tanks to collect and store rain channeled by gutters and pipes as it runs off the roofs of houses.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Despite an indigenous tradition of rain-water harvesting and irrigation systems going back to the third century BC, policy-makers in modern times have often overlooked the value of such technologies, and it is only recently that officials have taken much interest in household-level structures. Government and other programmes have, however, been top-down in their conception and application, installing tanks free of charge without providing training in the skills needed to build and maintain them properly. Practical Action South Asia's project deliberately took a different approach, aiming to build up a local skills base among builders and users of the tanks, and to create structures and systems so that communities can manage their own rainwater harvesting schemes.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The community of Muthukandiya was involved throughout. Two meetings were held where villagers analysed their water problems, developed a mitigation plan and selected the rainwater harvesting technology. Two local masons received several days' on-the-job training in building the 5,000-litre household storage tanks: surface tanks out of Ferro-cement and underground tanks out of brick. Each system, including tank, pipes, gutters and filters, cost US$195 –equivalent to a month's income for an average village family. Just over half the cost was provided by the community, in the form of materials and unskilled labour. Practical Action South Asia contributed the rest, including cement, transport and payment for the skilled labour. Households learned how to use and maintain the tanks, and the whole community was trained to keep domestic water supplies clean. A village rainwater harvesting society was set up to run the project. To date, 37 families in and around Muthukandiya have storage tanks. Evaluations show clearly that households with rainwater storage tanks have considerably more water for domestic needs than households relying entirely on wells and ponds. During the driest months, households with tanks may have up to twice as much water available. Their water is much cleaner, too.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Nandawathie, a widow in the village, has taken full advantage of the opportunities that rainwater harvesting has brought her family. With a better water supply now close at hand, she began by growing a few vegetables. The income from selling these helped her to open a small shop on her doorstep. This increased her earnings still further, enabling her to apply for a loan to install solar power in her house. She is now thinking of building another tank in her garden so that she can grow more vegetables. Nandawathie also feels safer now that she no longer has to fetch water from the village well in the early morning or late evening. She says that her children no longer complain so much of diarrhoea. And her daughter Sandamalee has more time for school work.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: In the short term, and on a small scale, the project has clearly been a success. The challenge lies in making such initiatives sustainable and expanding their coverage. At a purely technical level, rainwater harvesting is evidently sustainable. In Muthukandiya, the skills required to build and maintain storage tanks were taught fairly easily and can be shared by the two trained masons, who are now finding work with other development agencies in the district.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The non-structural elements of the work, especially it's financial and organizational, present a bigger challenge. A revolving fund was set up, with households that had already benefited agreeing to contribute a small monthly amount to pay for maintenance, repairs and new tanks. However, it appears that the revolving fund concept was not fully understood and it has proved difficult to get households to contribute. Recovering costs from interventions that do not generate income directly will always be a difficult proposition, although this can be overcome if the process is explained more fully at the outset.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: The Muthkandiya initiative was planned as a demonstration project, to show that community-based drought mitigation through rainwater harvesting was feasible. Several other organizations have begun their own projects using the same approach. The feasibility of introducing larger tanks is being investigated.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: However, a lot of effort and patience are needed to generate the interest, develop the skills and organize the management structures needed to implement sustainable community-based projects. It will probably be some time before rainwater harvesting technologies can spread rapidly and spontaneously across the district's villages, without external support.

questions.title: What is the major way for local people make barely support of living in Muthukandiya village?
questions.analysis:
题干要求我们找出在Muthukandiya村庄中，当地居民维持生计的主要方式是什么。

我们可以通过定位词"major way"来找到答案。在原文中，我们可以找到同义表达"relies on"，它出现在以下句子中："Because crop production, which relies entirely on rainfall, is insufficient to support most families, the village economy relies on men and women working as day-laborers in nearby sugar-cane plantations." 

这句话告诉我们，由于完全依赖于降雨的农作物产量不足以支持大多数家庭，村庄的经济依赖于男女在附近的甘蔗种植园工作。因此，农作物产量是当地居民维持生计的主要方式。

所以，答案是"Crop production"（农作物产量）。

questions.title: The cost of rainwater harvesting systems was shared by local villagers and the local government.
questions.analysis: 题干中提到"rainwater harvesting systems"的费用是由当地村民和地方政府共同承担的。在原文中，我们可以找到相关信息。原文中提到，整个社区参与了这个项目，包括分析水问题、制定缓解计划和选择雨水收集技术的会议。此外，还提到了两个当地泥瓦匠接受了几天的现场培训，建造了5000升的家庭储水罐。每个系统的成本是195美元，相当于一个普通村庄家庭一个月的收入。其中超过一半的费用由社区提供，以材料和非熟练劳动力的形式。剩下的费用由Practical Action South Asia提供，包括水泥、运输和熟练劳动力的支付。根据这些信息，我们可以得出结论，费用是由社区和Practical Action South Asia共同承担的，而不是地方政府。因此，答案是"NO"。

questions.title: Training two masons at the same time is much more preferable to training a single one.
questions.analysis:
题干中提到"Training two masons at the same time is much more preferable to training a single one."，我们需要在文章中找到相关信息来判断这个说法是否可以在文章中找到。在文章中，我们可以找到两个地方与题干相关。

首先，在第一段中提到"Two local masons received several days’ on-the-job training in building the 5,000-litre household storage tanks"，这表明有两个当地的泥瓦工接受了培训。这与题干中提到的同时培训两个泥瓦工的说法相符。

其次，在第二段中提到"In Muthukandiya, the skills required to build and maintain storage tanks were taught fairly easily and can be shared by the two trained masons"，这表明培训的泥瓦工学会了建造和维护储水罐的技能，并且这些技能可以被两个受过培训的泥瓦工分享。这也与题干中提到的同时培训两个泥瓦工的说法相符。

综上所述，文章中确实提到了同时培训两个泥瓦工的情况，因此答案应为"YES"或"TRUE"。

因此，这道题的答案不是"NOT GIVEN"，而是"YES"或"TRUE"。
